detection.layoutNotes: Questions are spread across two pages with an objectives mapping table at the bottom of the final page. Multi-part questions (a, b, c, d) have been combined into a single question entry because they all assess the same objective.

markingStyle.notes: Tutors mark with ticks and crosses next to each question.

objectivesSection.rawText:
Question 1: Find the mean for a small set of discrete data
Question 2: Calculate the median of a set of data
Question 3: Extract data and interpret line graphs
Question 4: Calculate the mean for a small set of discrete data
Question 5: Interpret data from simple compound and comparative bar charts
Question 6: Read and draw tally charts, tables, charts and line graphs, including for grouped data
Question 7: Compare sets of data using their ranges and averages

items.objectiveText: Find the mean for a small set of discrete data
Calculate the median of a set of data
Extract data and interpret line graphs
Calculate the mean for a small set of discrete data
Interpret data from simple compound and comparative bar charts
Read and draw tally charts, tables, charts and line graphs, including for grouped data
Compare sets of data using their ranges and averages

questions.label: Q1
questions.objective: Find the mean for a small set of discrete data
questions.questionSummary: Calculate the mean of a small data set and explain how adding a value of zero affects the mean.
questions.detectionHints: Ticks or crosses are typically placed beside parts (a) and (b), with the working and final answers marked.

questions.label: Q2
questions.objective: Calculate the median of a set of data
questions.questionSummary: Find the median, mean and range of a list of discrete data.
questions.detectionHints: Each sub-part is usually marked individually beside the answer spaces.

questions.label: Q3
questions.objective: Extract data and interpret line graphs
questions.questionSummary: Use the graph to determine the mode and the range.
questions.detectionHints: Marks normally appear beside parts (a) and (b).

questions.label: Q4
questions.objective: Calculate the mean for a small set of discrete data
questions.questionSummary: Calculate the mean and range of measured lengths, then determine the new mean and range after every value increases by 1 cm.
questions.detectionHints: Each calculation part is marked separately next to the answer lines.

questions.label: Q5
questions.objective: Interpret data from simple compound and comparative bar charts
questions.questionSummary: Interpret a compound bar chart and compare calls made with calls received.
questions.detectionHints: Tutors usually mark the identified day and the written explanation separately.

questions.label: Q6
questions.objective: Read and draw tally charts, tables, charts and line graphs, including for grouped data
questions.questionSummary: Complete a grouped frequency table from a list of ages using equal class intervals.
questions.detectionHints: Marks are generally awarded across the completed tally and frequency table.

questions.label: Q7
questions.objective: Compare sets of data using their ranges and averages
questions.questionSummary: Compare two classes using their means and ranges.
questions.detectionHints: Marks are normally given for each valid comparison in the written response.
